metadata.description: Manuscript reference cleanup + surgical edits for Nature Collection (PALCOMMS) submission. Execute audit decisions from 2026-04-14 handoff: drop Mohr 2020, drop Törnberg & Uitermark 2024, rework Crawford 2021 to engage classification argument. Then drop H1/H2/H3 Results numbering, convert bullets to prose, tighten policy bullets, regenerate .docx files, verify 191 tests. Deadline April 30, 2026.

summary:
Completed NatCol manuscript submission-polish pass: dropped Mohr 2020 + Törnberg & Uitermark 2024, reworked Crawford 2021 into a specific classification-politics insertion, removed H1/H2/H3 Results markers, converted Results bullet list to prose, renamed 3 Territory/Map subheadings to plain alternatives, tightened Implications for Policy bullets to one prose sentence, dropped old Figure 3 and promoted the 6-panel compression-metrics figure to new Figure 3. Regenerated main/anonymous/SI .docx files. 191/191 tests pass. Reference count: 57 → 54.

WHAT IS NEXT for the next session:
1. Decide whether to port these same edits to NMS2026 parallel version (currently un-synced). If yes, the edits are mechanical text replacements identical to what was applied to NatCol — body text between named and anonymous is character-identical except author metadata.
2. Decide on the two residual inline map/territory metaphors that were left in place per user scoping: one in Discussion §Agenda Misalignment ("The public discourse 'map' does not allocate proportional representational space to this stakeholder 'territory.'"), one in Conclusion penultimate paragraph ("risks legislating for a 'map' that does not match the 'territory.'"). If "one metaphor per paper" full consistency is wanted, rewrite both.
3. Final pre-submission checklist items from HANDOFF (EPS/TIFF conversion, UK English sweep, anonymized URL verification) still pending.
4. Figure 1 variant selection still pending user pick (5 variants exist in figures/manuscript/).
5. Deadline: **April 30, 2026** (16 days out).

decision.description: Execute all 8 substantive edits (drop Mohr 2020, drop Törnberg & Uitermark 2024, rework Crawford 2021, strip H1/H2/H3 markers, bullets→prose, drop Territory/Map subheadings, tighten policy bullets, drop current Figure 3 and promote current Figure 4 to new Figure 3) on the Nature Collection manuscript (named + anonymous), its SUPPLEMENTARY_INFORMATION, and GitHub repo documentation. Defer NMS2026 parallel edits unless user instructs otherwise. Regenerate .docx files and verify tests at the end.
decision.rationale: User confirmed all style choices (Territory/Map subheadings drop, policy bullet tightening) and added figure renumber (drop current Fig 3, promote Fig 4 → Fig 3). User explicitly scoped target to "Nature Collection" manuscript + corresponding SI + GitHub repo documentation.

context.conversation_snippet: "i also agree with the 2 style choices and want you to implement them now aliong with the other 1-6 steps so we can do all changes at once. and be sure that you are changing the Nature Collection manuscript + corresponding supp info + github repo documentation etc"

contribution.description: Executed full submission-polish pass on NatCol manuscript: dropped Mohr 2020 (inline + bib), dropped Törnberg & Uitermark 2024 (inline + bib), reworked Crawford 2021 to a specific classification-politics sentence that foreshadows Bowker & Star, stripped three H1/H2/H3 bold confirmation markers from Results, converted T13/T1/T7/T3 bullet list to prose, renamed 3 map/territory subheadings to plain alternatives, rewrote the opening sentence of §Public Discourse to remove the map/territory echo, tightened the 4-bullet Implications for Policy list into one prose sentence, dropped old Figure 3 (macro-theme comparison), promoted old Figure 4 (6-panel compression metrics) to new Figure 3, redirected the inline "(Figure 3)" ref in Results §H1 to "(Figure 1)". Applied identically to named + anonymous markdown. Regenerated all 3 .docx files. 191/191 tests pass.

context.conversation_snippet: "i also agree with the 2 style choices and want you to implement them now aliong with the other 1-6 steps so we can do all changes at once. and be sure that you are changing the Nature Collection manuscript + corresponding supp info + github repo documentation etc"

contribution.description: Updated HANDOFF_NEXT_SESSION.md top-of-file with the 8 completed actions from this session (reference drops, H1/H2/H3 cleanup, subheading renames, policy bullet tightening, figure renumber), flagged two remaining inline map/territory metaphors (Discussion §Agenda Misalignment and Conclusion) as out-of-scope for this pass, noted NMS2026 parallel port is deferred, and adjusted the reference count from 57 to 54.

annotation.content: The body text of MANUSCRIPT_NatureCollection2026.md and MANUSCRIPT_NatureCollection2026_ANONYMOUS.md is character-identical except for authorship metadata (author names, institution, corresponding email, repository URL). Substantive edits can therefore be applied identically to both files in parallel with the same old_string/new_string pairs. Verified at lines 1-15 (differs — metadata) and lines 125-135, 410-418 (identical — body text).
